sections.label: Section 1419
sections.translation: Bury my children as I said .

sections.label: Section 1419b
sections.translation: But who will bury me , my son ?

sections.label: Section 1420c
sections.translation: After you have buried my children .

sections.label: Section 1421b
sections.original:
εἰς Ἀθήνας πέμψομαι Θηβῶν ἄπο .
ἀλλʼ ἐσκόμιζε τέκνα δυσκόμιστα γῇ ·
ἡμεῖς δʼ ἀναλώσαντες αἰσχύναις δόμον ,
Θησεῖ πανώλεις ἑψόμεσθʼ ἐφολκίδες .
ὅστις δὲ πλοῦτον ἢ σθένος μᾶλλον φίλων
sections.translation: I will fetch you from Thebes to Athens . But carry my children within , a grievous burden to the earth . And I , after ruining my house by deeds of shame , will follow as a little boat in the wake of Theseus , a total wreck .

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.translation: Whoever prefers wealth or might to the possession of good friends , thinks wrongly .

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original:
στείχομεν οἰκτροὶ καὶ πολύκλαυτοι ,
τὰ μέγιστα φίλων ὀλέσαντες .
sections.translation: With grief and many a bitter tear we go our way , robbed of all we prized most dearly .
